method: HARDENING/robustness of the 2D-ruled-grid finding (E079 horizontal glyph-spacing + E080 vertical line-spacing). Re-ran BOTH E079/E080 metrics + nulls across a row-clustering threshold sweep {0.5,0.6,0.7}x median-glyph-height + an independent relative-gap row-detection method, globally + per-site (HT/Khania/Zakros), with a 2-arm synthetic PC (detect regular grid / reject random layout). Frozen verdict from the sweep + PC. L2, opaque IDs, positions only.
result: The raw threshold-sweep is ROBUST (both S_h and S_v stay significant + regular at all 4 row-detection settings, 3/3 sites; verified independently by the coordinator), BUT the epoch's false-positive positive-control FAILED (random-layout synthetics flagged as regular 0.20 of the time, worker; the coordinator's independent stricter reconstruction gives up to 1.00), because the pitch-CV metric conflates deliberate ruled-grid spacing with fixed-glyph-width regularization (pitch = gap + glyph-width; uniform widths regularize pitch even in a random layout). Per the FROZEN rule (PC-fail -> MACHINERY_UNINFORMATIVE), this epoch does NOT certify robustness. E079/E080 are UNAFFECTED — they rest on their own passed PITCH-LEVEL PCs, not on this glyph-level one. E082 surfaces an honest interpretive CAVEAT on E079: part of the horizontal-spacing regularity may reflect uniform glyph widths (E076) rather than purely deliberate even spacing.

coordinator_fp_reconstruction.note: stricter glyph-level random-layout synthetic; confirms the metric is anti-conservative for width-regularized random layouts -> PC genuinely uncalibratable at the glyph level; NOT an E080-style artifact (which would have IMPROVED under the correct construction).

key_findings: Raw threshold-robustness CONFIRMED + coordinator-verified: S_h = 0.333/0.338/0.375 and S_v = 0.119/0.111/0.119 at tol 0.5/0.6/0.7 (alt-gap: S_h 0.367, S_v 0.511), all perm_p ~ 0.002, all << the E079/E080 nulls (~0.76 h, ~0.36 v); per-site HT/Khania/Zakros all significant for both metrics. The 2D-grid's raw statistics are NOT an artifact of the 0.6 row-clustering threshold.

The false-positive PC FAILED (0.20 worker; up to 1.00 on the coordinator's stricter reconstruction) -> MACHINERY_UNINFORMATIVE. The pitch-CV metric conflates ruled-grid regularity with fixed-glyph-width regularization, so a glyph-level random-layout null cannot be cleanly rejected. This is a REAL calibration limit, NOT an E080-style construction artifact (independent reconstruction made it worse, not better).

E079/E080 are UNAFFECTED: their own PCs used PITCH-LEVEL random synthetics (proper) and passed; the E082 failure is specific to the GLYPH-LEVEL construction. But E082 surfaces an honest CAVEAT on E079's interpretation: some of the horizontal spacing regularity may reflect uniform glyph widths (E076 shared-size) rather than purely deliberate even spacing.

alt-gap row-detection weakens v-regularity (S_v 0.511 vs ~0.11 under thresholds) though still significant -> the vertical-line regularity is somewhat row-detection-method-dependent, a secondary caveat.
successor_hypotheses: Re-test E079 with a WIDTH-CONTROLLED metric: gap-only spacing (edge-to-edge, removing glyph width) vs a random-gap null, to separate deliberate even spacing from width-regularization.
Build a PC whose random-layout synthetic uses REAL LA glyph-width distributions (variable widths) to see if the false-positive rate drops below 0.10 (fair calibration).
Test whether the vertical-line regularity (E080) survives the relative-gap row-detection with its OWN null (S_v 0.511 there needs its matched null to interpret).
Quantify how much of E079's pitch-CV is width-driven vs gap-driven (decompose pitch = gap + width variance).
Apply the width-controlled spacing metric cross-site to re-confirm or qualify the E079 horizontal-grid claim.
non_circular: SigLA per-glyph bbox positions only; reused the E079 (random-composition) + E080 (clustering-conditional) nulls verbatim; opaque sign IDs, no value/reading; the PC failure was independently reconstructed by the coordinator (made worse, confirming a real calibration limit, not an artifact).
deviations: WORKER FINALIZATION FAILURE + COORDINATOR RECOVERY: the GLM worker computed the full analysis (run_output.json) but STALLED writing result.json (~20 min, same failure mode as the v1 attempt which thrashed 58 steps/932K tokens). The coordinator killed the stalled worker, independently VERIFIED the sweep (exact match) and ADJUDICATED the PC failure (reconstruction made FP worse, 0.20->1.00, confirming a genuine calibration limit), and finalized result.json from the worker's own computed run_output.json. GLM did the labor; the coordinator verified + recovered the finalization. See epochs/EPOCH-082/COORDINATOR_NOTE.md.
v1 of this epoch (6 settings x 2000-draw nulls) was too heavy and thrashed; v2 (scaffolded, 4 settings, 500-draw nulls) completed the computation but the worker still could not finalize result.json.
SigLA bbox JSON is licensed-derived + gitignored (regenerable via scripts/sigla_decode.py); epoch prereg/plan_hash/machinery/result/report/COORDINATOR_NOTE ARE committed.
